AGENDA FOR
MARIN COUNTY BOARD OF SUPERVISORS MEETING


Tuesday, June 10, 2025
Board of Supervisors Chambers, Room 330, Civic Center

Board of Supervisors meetings are conducted in Board Chambers, Room 330, Marin County Civic Center, for the public to attend in-person.

Meetings are also streamed live, and public members may participate remotely, as described below.

How to Watch the Meeting:
  • Online: Marin County Meeting Archive or CMCM TV Live.
  • Please note that there may be a delay when you view the livestream.
  • Local television: tune in to Comcast Channel 27 and AT&T Channel 99.

Spanish translation, including public comment translation, will be available for all our Board meetings. To access the Spanish language audio today, you need to connect with Zoom and select language Spanish. We invite members of the public to access our Board of Supervisors’ Current Agenda website for additional information and instructions regarding public comment in English or Spanish.

How to Provide Comment on Agenda Items:
  • Before the meeting: email your comments to BOS@marincounty.gov. Please email your comments no later than noon the Monday before the Tuesday Board meeting to facilitate timely distribution to Board members. Please include the agenda item number you are addressing and your name and address. Your comments will be forwarded to the Board of Supervisors and placed into the public record.
  • During the meeting (only): To comment on an agenda item during the meeting while participating remotely, please follow the instructions at Marin County Meeting Archive.

If inappropriate (hate speech) public comment occurs that causes a disruption, the President may instruct the Zoom provider to mute the caller if unable to restore order.

Remote Zoom participation for members of the public is provided for convenience only. In the event that the Zoom connection malfunctions for any reason, the Board of Supervisors reserves the right to conduct the meeting without remote access.

Respectful Dialogue: The Marin County Board of Supervisors encourages a respectful dialogue that supports freedom of speech and values diversity of opinion. The Board, staff, and members of the public are expected to be civil and courteous, and to refrain from questioning the character or motives of others participating in the meeting. The County requests that speakers not use threatening, profane, or abusive language which disrupts, disturbs, or otherwise impedes the orderly conduct of the Board meeting. Members of the Public may comment on any item on the agenda during Board consideration of the item. The Board President will invite public comment following the staff presentation and prior to final Board deliberations. Each speaker will be allotted time for comment as set by the Board President (generally 2 minutes).

Levine Act Compliance: Effective January 1, 2023,the Levine Act (Government Code Section 84308) governs contributions in Board of Supervisor proceedings related to licenses, permits, or entitlements, as defined. Board members are prohibited from accepting contributions exceeding $500 from parties, their agents, or participants during such proceedings and for 12 months after a final decision. If a Board member received a contribution exceeding $500 within the past 12 months, disclosure is required on the record, and the member must not use their position to influence decisions. Parties and participants must disclose applicable contributions exceeding $500 on the record, providing the following:

• Name of the party or participant and any other person making the contribution

• The name of the recipient

• The amount of the contribution; and

• The date the contribution was made.

This information does not constitute legal advice. Parties and participants are advised to consult their legal counsel for guidance on compliance.

9:00 a.m. Convened as the Marin County Board of Supervisors.

1. Request to adopt a resolution recognizing and celebrating June 19, 2025, as Juneteenth Independence Day in Marin County. (EST. TIME 25 MIN.)
Recommended action: Adopt resolution.

1 - CEREMONIAL RESOLUTION

2. Open time

3. Board of Supervisors’ matters and informational updates concerning regional Board assignments and County Executive’s report. (EST TIME 30 MIN.)  Note: Public comment for Consent Calendars A and B will be heard together prior to the Board acting on the consent calendars.

4. Consent Calendar A (Items CA-1 through CA-9)

CA-1. Board of Supervisors

a. Request for In Memoriam adjournments as requested by Board Members during Board of Supervisors’ matters.

b. Approval of minutes of the meeting of May 6, 2025.

CA-1b - MINUTES

c. Request to adopt a resolution commending Dr. Alan Chee, DDS, as the 2025 Community Health Champion.

CA-1c - CEREMONIAL RESOLUTION

d. Request to adopt a resolution commending Dr. José A. Chibrás-Sainz as 2025 Community Health Champion.

CA-1d - CEREMONIAL RESOLUTION

e. Request to adopt a resolution commending Stacy Scott as the 2025 Community Health Champion.

CA-1e - CEREMONIAL RESOLUTION

f. Request to adopt a resolution commending Don Dickenson for 22 Years of Service on the Marin County Planning Commission.

CA-1f - CEREMONIAL RESOLUTION

g. Recruitments. (The below appointments are open for recruitment. For more information about current vacancies, qualifications needed for open seats, and how to apply, visit the Boards and Commissions webpage.) Request to schedule the following appointments:

i. Child Care Commission One vacancy due to the resignation of Ginny Kuhel (SUPT-Comm).

CA-2. Community Development Agency

a. Request to authorize the President of the Board of Supervisors to execute Amendment 1 to the agreement with Canal Alliance, increasing the Community Development Block Grant award for the Marin Villas Phase 2 Renovation Project located at 153 Novato Street, San Rafael, CA 94901 by $200,000, for a total contract amount of $380,000, subject to County Counsel approval.

CA-2a - ATTACHMENT

CA-2a - AGREEMENT

b. Request to authorize the President to review and approve (i) an Assignment and Assumption Agreement of the Grant Agreement in the amount of $648,672 from Marin County’s Affordable Housing Trust Fund; (ii) an amended Grant Agreement in the amount of $648,672 from Marin County’s Affordable Housing Trust Fund; (iii) a Grant Agreement in the amount of $625,000 of Measure W Funds; and (iv) Regulatory Agreements for Housing Trust and Measure W funds with Habitat for Humanity Greater San Francisco to support the development of affordable housing at the property located at 31 Wharf Road, Bolinas.

CA-2b - AGREEMENT 1

CA-2b - AGREEMENT 2

CA-2b - AGREEMENT 3

CA-2b - AGREEMENT 4

c. Request to authorize the President, subject to County Counsel approval, to execute Amendment 1 to the agreement between the County of Marin and EAH Housing, increasing the total Community Development Block Grant contract amount by $617,825, bringing the total allocation to $839,000, to support the Point Reyes Affordable Homes Rehabilitation Project.

CA-2c - ATTACHMENT

CA-2c - AGREEMENT

d. Request to authorize the President to (i) execute Fifth Addendum to Professional Services Agreement with Sicular Environmental Consulting & Natural Lands Management for an increase of $10,145; and (ii) approve a contract time extension to June 30, 2026.

CA-2d - STAFF REPORT

CA-2d - AGREEMENT

a. Request to authorize the President to approve the FY 25/26 Annual Statement of Investment Policy & the Marin County Long-Term Investment Pool Policy.

CA-3a - ATTACHMENT 2

b. Request to authorize the President to receive and approve the Monthly Report of County, Schools, and District Investments as of April 30, 2025.

CA-5. Fire

a. Request to authorize the President to execute a Memorandum of Understanding between Marin County’s Office of Emergency Management and West Marin Fund to establish two dedicated charitable funds for disaster-related financial donations – Disaster Preparedness Fund and Disaster Recovery Fund.

CA-5a - AGREEMENT

Request to authorize the President to approve participation in the Calendar Year 2024 California Department of Health Care Services for Marin County Fire Department contributions to the Variable Rate Range Program supporting reimbursement funding for Medicare services; and authorize the Marin County Fire Chief to be the Authorized Agent for the agreement.

CA-6. Health and Human Services

b. Request to authorize the President to approve the First Addendum to the $232,989 St. Vincent de Paul Society’s Shallow Rent Subsidies for Older Adults contract, amending the contract term to start March 1, 2025, and extend through June 30, 2027.

CA-6c - AGREEMENT

d. Request to authorize the President to execute the First Addendum to the FY 2024-25 contract with Cappstone, Inc., increasing the contract amount by $10,500 for a new total amount of $102,851 for janitorial services at 250 Bon Air Road.

CA-6d - STAFF REPORT

CA-6d - AGREEMENT

e. Request to Authorize the President to delegate signatory authority to the Director of Health and Human Services, or designee, to sign the FY 2025-27 Memorandum of Understanding (#2024-1464-A00) and any subsequent amendments and related documents with Sonoma County in an amount of $1,100,000 for Behavioral Health Bridge Housing services at 440 Arrowood Drive.

CA-6e - STAFF REPORT

CA-6e - AGREEMENT

f. Request to authorize the purchase of a mobile van from LDV Custom Specialty Vehicles in an amount not to exceed $514,321, inclusive of a 10% contingency amount to serve residents in West Marin.

g. Request to authorize the President to approve the First Addendum to the two-year FY 2023-25 Contract with SVT Gruppe, Inc. to increase the contract amount by $10,000 to $60,000 for Lanterman-Petris-Short Conservatee transportation services.

CA-6g - AGREEMENT

h. Request to authorize the President to approve the Third Addendum to the FY 2022-2025 contract with St. Vincent de Paul Society of Marin increasing the contract amount by $50,000 to a new total of $1,050,000 and extending the contract term to August 31, 2025, to provide services for the Adult Protective Services Home Safe Program.

CA-6h - AGREEMENT

CA-7. Library

a. Request to authorize the President to approve the contract with Facilities By Design of Santa Rosa, CA in the amount of $150,000 for furniture management services associated with the Marin County Free Library 10-Branch Library Refresh Measure B Project (54LP16RFMB) for an initial contract term beginning in June 2025 and ending in August 2026.

5. Consent Calendar B (Items CB-1 through CB-7)

CB-1. Community Development Agency

a. Request to authorize the President to execute the Third Addendum to the contract with TRB + Associates to provide overflow plan review support for building permit customers to receive timely plan reviews for their building permit applications, increasing the contract by $250,000 for a new maximum amount not to exceed $549,000 and to extend the contract term to August 31, 2028.

CB-1a - STAFF REPORT

CB-1a - AGREEMENT

b. Request to authorize the President to (i) approve a grant in the amount of $500,000 from the Restricted Affordable Housing Fund to capitalize the loan pool administered by Marin Housing Authority, to be used for the preservation and expansion of the County’s Below Market Rate housing portfolio; (ii) review and approve change in commitment to Marin Housing Authority of a grant in the amount of $150,000 from Marin County’s Affordable Housing Trust Fund which was originally awarded to Two Valleys Community Land Trust for the property located at 6956 Sir Francis Drake Boulevard, Forest Knolls; (iii) review and approve change in commitment to Marin Housing Authority of a grant in the amount of $150,000 of Measure W funds which was originally awarded to Two Valleys Community Land Trust for the property located at 6956 Sir Francis Drake Boulevard, Forest Knolls; (iv) authorize President to execute the following: (a) Assignment and Assumption Agreement (b) County Housing Grant Agreement; (c) Measure W Grant Agreement (d) Regulatory Agreement; and (v) Approve budget adjustments as outlined in the staff report dated June 10, 2025.

CB-1b - ATTACHMENT 2

CB-1b - ATTACHMENT 3

CB-1b - ATTACHMENT 4

CB-1b - ATTACHMENT 5

CB-1c - AGREEMENT

d. Request to review and approve Resolution 2025-56 authorizing the County of Marin to accept and manage the $400,000 Association of Bay Area Governments Transit Oriented Communities Housing Policy Grant on behalf of Marin County’s Transit Oriented Communities located in cities and towns, that may include: San Rafael, Novato, Sausalito, Larkspur; and approve related budget adjustment as detailed in the staff report dated June 10, 2025.

CB-1d - STAFF REPORT

CB-1d - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-56

e. Request to authorize the President to execute the Third Addendum to the contract with CSG Consultants to ensure continuity to provide responsive inspection services to customers during periods of staff absence or position vacancy, increasing the contract by $250,000 for a new maximum amount not to exceed $549,000 and to extend the contract term to August 31, 2028.

CB-1e - STAFF REPORT

CB-1e - AGREEMENT

a. Request to approve the addition of a 1.0 full-time equivalent – Business System Analyst (0339) position to the Department of Finance Fiscal Administrative Services Unit (10043591) and, upon completion of a department promotional recruitment and hiring process, delete a vacated 1.0 full-time equivalent.

CB-4. Parks
Request to authorize the Director of Parks to collaborate with Human Resources and the Office of the County Executive to implement the position adjustments outlined in this report, all of which are cost-covered and designed to improve organizational efficiency, program delivery, and alignment with strategic departmental priorities.

CB-6. Public Defender

CB-6 - STAFF REPORT

CB-7. Public Works

a. Request to (i) authorize the Department of Finance to increase expenditure appropriations in the Capital Projects Fund (1420) Civic Center Roof Cleaning Project (41C2405) by $603,954; (ii) award a construction contract for the Civic Center Roof Cleaning Project (41C2405) to the lowest responsible bidder, Rainbow Waterproofing and Restoration Company, in the amount of $1,496,365; (iii) authorize a 10% contingency of $149,636 for a total construction cost of $1,646,001; and (iv) authorize the Interim Public Works Director to execute the construction contract.

CB-7a - STAFF REPORT

CB-7a - AGREEMENT

b. Request to (i) authorize the Department of Finance to increase expenditure appropriations in the Road and Bridge Fund (1430) 2025 Road Sealant Project (41EP2422) by $3,872,152; (ii) award the 2025 Road Sealant Project (41EP2422) construction contract to the lowest responsible bidder, Dryco Construction, Inc., in the amount of $3,229,229; (iii) authorize a 10% contract contingency of $322,923; and (iv) authorize the Director of Public Works or their designee to execute the construction contract.

CB-7b - STAFF REPORT

CB-7b - AGREEMENT

c. Request to approve purchase of two Hi-Tech Type III/International Fire Engines through Hi-Tech Emergency Vehicle Service, Inc. of Oakdale, CA for a cost not to exceed $1,585,695; and authorize the Director of Finance to approve appropriating $1,585,695 of Fund 6620 as detailed in the staff report dated June 10, 2025.

CB-7c - STAFF REPORT

d. Request to (i) authorize the Department of Finance to increase expenditure appropriations in the Department of Public Works 99 Binford fund by $100,000.00; and (ii) authorize the President of the Board to execute the Addendum to the general service agreement with The Other Junk CO., in the amount of $100,000, for a revised total agreement of $350,000.

CB-7d - STAFF REPORT

CB-7d - ATTACHMENT

CB-7d - AGREEMENT

6. Request from the Department of Health and Human Services to adopt resolutions establishing the FY 2025-26 Schedule of Charges for community mental health system of care services and also county-operated substance use treatment services. (EST TIME 25 MIN.)
Recommended action: Adopt a resolution establishing the FY 2025-26 Schedule of Charges for county-operated Mental Health System of care services; and adopt a resolution establishing the FY 2025-26 Schedule of Charges for county-operated Substance Use treatment services.

6 - STAFF REPORT

6 - ATTACHMENT 1

6 - ATTACHMENT 2

6 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-50

6 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-51

10:32 a.m.

7. Request from the County Executive to conduct a hearing on Tax-exempt financing by the California Statewide Communities Development Authority; Consent to hold a Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act public hearing on June 10, 2025 regarding the issuance of up to $165,000,000 of tax-exempt conduit debt to finance and refinance facilities known as The Tamalpais Marin, in connection with the provision of senior residential and care services located at 501 Via Casitas, Greenbrae, California 94904 ("Sequoia Living" or the "Borrower"). (EST TIME 5 MIN.)
Recommended actions: Conduct a hearing and adopt the resolution approving the tax-exempt and taxable financing and the issuance of the obligations by California Statewide Communities Development Authority for Sequoia Living.

7 - STAFF REPORT

7 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-52

7 - NOTICE

8. Request from the Department of Public Works to receive an update on San Geronimo Fire Department Facilities Project Delivery Method, Authorization to Release Request for Proposals for Owner’s Representative Consulting Services. (EST TIME 45 MIN.)
Recommended actions: Receive an update on the New San Geronimo Fire Facilities and Project Delivery Method Recommendation; and authorize the release of a Request for Proposals and selection of Owner's Representative Consulting Services for the San Geronimo New Fire Facilities Project.

8 - STAFF REPORT

8 - ATTACHMENT 1

8 - ATTACHMENT 2

8 - ATTACHMENT 3

8 - PRESENTATION

Recess to Closed Session as the Marin County Board of Supervisors at 11:20 a.m.

9. Closed Session (EST TIME 90 MIN.)

a. CONFERENCE WITH LABOR NEGOTIATOR California Government Code section 54957.6 Agency Negotiators: Christina Cramer, Human Resources Director, Lisa Li Employee Labor Relations Director, Charles Sakai, Sloan Sakai Yeung & Wong Employee Organization(s): All

d. CONFERENCE WITH REAL PROPERTY NEGOTIATOR[S] California Government Code section 54956.8 Real Property Address: 6th and B Streets in Point Reyes Station (APN 119-198-09) Agency Negotiator(s): Christy McRoy, Principal Real Property Agent Negotiating Parties: House in Point Reyes LLC; Community Land Trust of West Marin Under Negotiation: Price and terms of payment

9d - STAFF REPORT

Announcement from Closed Session.

The Board reconvened in Open Session at 1:32 P.M.

President Sackett had nothing to report.

1:30 p.m.

10. Request from the Office of the County Executive to give a presentation and adopt a resolution to appoint members to the Sheriff’s Civilian Oversight Committee. (EST TIME 90 MIN.)
Recommended actions: Approve a resolution to appoint nine members to the Civilian Oversight Commission as detailed in the staff report dated June 10, 2025.

10 - STAFF REPORT

10 - RESOLUTION 2025-47

10 - PRESENTATION

10 - PUBLIC COMMENT

10 - LATE PUBLIC COMMENT

11. Request from Community Development Agency to hold a public hearing to approve Consolidated Plan for FY 2025-2029, including Budgets for the Community Development Block Grant and HOME Investment Partnerships Programs and funding recommendations for the 2025 Calendar Year Permanent Local Housing Allocation as detailed in the staff report dated June 10, 2025. (EST. TIME 30 MINS.)
Recommended actions: (i)  Adopt a resolution authorizing staff to submit the 2025-2029 Consolidated Plan and the 2025-2026 Annual Action Plan to the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development; and (ii) adopt a resolution approving funding allocations of Marin County's Affordable Housing Fund matched with Permanent Local Housing Allocation Funds.

11 - STAFF REPORT

11 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-48

11 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-49

11 - ATTACHMENT 3

11 - ATTACHMENT 4

11 - ATTACHMENT 5

11 - ATTACHMENT 6

11 - ATTACHMENT 7

11 - PRESENTATION

12. Request from Community Development Agency to adopt a Resolution Issuing a Notice of Intent to Purchase the Vacant Land located at B and 6th Street in Point Reyes Station (APN 119-198-09) from Community Land Trust of West Marin. (EST. TIME 30 MINS.)
Recommended action: (i) Approve and authorize the President to execute the Resolution; (ii) direct the Clerk of the Board to publish a Notice of Intent to Purchase Real Property three times in the Marin Independent Journal, the first time on June 13, 2025, the second time on June 20, 2025, and the third time on June 27, 2025.

12 - STAFF REPORT

12 - RESOLUTION NO. 2025-46

12 - NOTICE

12 - PUBLIC COMMENT

12 - LATE PUBLIC COMMENT

13. Request from Human Resources to recognize 2024 employee length of service anniversaries with the County of Marin for 20, 25, 30, and 35 years. (EST. TIME 60 MINS.)
Recommended action: Conduct a ceremony.

13 - STAFF REPORT

13 - PRESENTATION 1

13 - PRESENTATION 2

Adjourn as Marin County Board of Supervisors at 4:10 p.m.
